TEXT.English: Shows the "Glance" tab (relations matrix) on the Foreign Advisor screen. (This tab had been briefly disabled for aesthetic reasons by a BtS patch.)
When checked, hides the Great Person bulbing indicators on the Technology Advisor screen. Note: indicators appear after turn 0 due to an engine bug.
When checked, disables the BUG Religion Advisor (sortable city table with religious building info) and uses the BtS screen instead.

When checked, the name of the mod's folder is written into your replay files, which excludes them from the Hall of Fame screen when BtS is run without mods. (As for showing replays created by BtS or other mods on the AdvCiv Hall of Fame screen, see the [COLOR_BUILDING_TEXT]HOF_...[COLOR_REVERT] variables in [COLOR_BUILDING_TEXT]GlobalDefines_advc.xml[COLOR_REVERT].)
When checked, the shown odds ignore free wins against Barbarians that the handicap level may guarantee. If not checked, the shown odds are 100% when a free win applies.

When checked, your player name is used for the saved game name along with the turn year. Otherwise the name is a concatenation of 3-letter versions of Leader Name, Handicap, Map Size, Map Type, Speed, Era, Year, Climate and Sea Level.
Enter the user defined naming convention for combat units 'AIR_BOMBER'.
Enter the user defined naming convention for combat units 'AIR_FIGHTER'.
Enter the user defined naming convention for combat units 'ARCHER_BOW_SHORT'.
Enter the user defined naming convention for combat units 'ARCHER_BOW_LONG'.
Enter the user defined naming convention for combat units 'ARCHER_CROSSBOW'.
Enter the user defined naming convention for combat units 'MOUNTED MELEE'.
Enter the user defined naming convention for combat units 'MOUNTED RANGED'.
Terrains (Minus some Plot types) with no Feature
Terrains Only If With This Bonus's Featur.
Bonuses in this terrain only if the terrain has no feature (see sevopedia bonus)
Bonuses in this plot type (bHills enabled in these bonuses's XML)
Bonuses (Not applicable for this plot type / terrain)
Excluded from display (as is inaccurate or incomplete for this plot type / terrain)
Bonuses in this terrain only if the bonus's features are compatible with "terrains only if with this feature" (see sevopedia bonus)
Bonuses On Any Terrain (see also sevopedia bonus)
Note: Civ selection/action sounds can include several variants. Replay to hear different samples.
Civilizations that start with this technology
[ICON_BULLET]Raises [ICON_DEFENSE] to %d1_Mod%% against All Units (using now bIgnoreBuildingDefense 0 (for all units? Not sure.))
No resolution yet: requires one of the below.
No voting members yet: requires one of the below.
Note: the tech tree does not list obsoleted units to avoid clutter.
Against Classes (A/D), Combat T. (A and D)
Of Other Classes's Units 's (A/D) and Combat Types's Units (A and D) 's Modifiers Against This Unit

[TAB]Sometimes called the "clubman" (as it wields a wooden club), the Ancient Maceman is a durable and quite fast Stone Age unit, so can be used to explore the map with less to fear from predators such as Lions and Bears.
Cannot move (outside of cultural borders): units with TerrainImpassables or TerrainPassableTechs in their XML
Cannot move: Domain Land/Air without bCanMoveImpassable / bCanMoveAllTerrain / FeaturePassableTech; Domain Sea without bCanMoveAllTerrain in their XML
Cannot move (outside of cultural borders): units with FeatureImpassables or FeaturePassableTechs in their XML
Cannot move: Domain Land/Air without bCanMoveAllTerrain; Domain Sea without bCanMoveImpassable / bCanMoveAllTerrain / FeaturePassableTech in their XML
[ICON_BULLET]Provides power ([ICON_POWER]) with [COLOR_HIGHLIGHT_TEXT][LINK=literal]%s1_BonusName[\LINK][COLOR_REVERT] (from any city as long as this bonus is connected to your trade network)
(clean power ([ICON_POWER])) for All Cities on this Continent:

; as for in this City specifically rather instead, i (advciv-sas developer/maintainer) don't know if clean in all cities overrides dirty in this city specifically or if if dirty in this city specifically adds up (only in this city) to clean power health change from all cities so showing both for this city, so in this city clean power ([ICON_POWER]) (or dirty power ([ICON_POWER]) ?):
[ICON_BULLET]Cannot Enter (only applies to tiles outside of cultural borders. This effect applies to these tiles even if they are roaded):
"We fear you are trading more than us."
Special Abilities with some AI information
As a reminder, the following technologies cannot be traded:

%s14%d1_Pts[TAB]from sum of scored chapters [NEWLINE]%s15%d2_Pts[TAB]from finished but unscored chapters[NEWLINE]%s16+%d3_Pts[TAB]from victory, compensating for %d4 missed chapters[NEWLINE]%s17 -%d5_Pts[TAB]from initial rank (%d6. of %d7)[NEWLINE]%s18+%d8_Pts[TAB]for %d9%% time unspent in current chapter[NEWLINE]%s19+%d10_Pts[TAB](+%d11%%) from %s12_Hnd handicap[NEWLINE] -----------------------------------------------------------[NEWLINE]%s20 %d13_Pts[TAB]in total
Game Handicap (AI discounts):[NEWLINE][TAB]%s1_GameHandicap[NEWLINE]Human Player Handicap: %s2_HumanPlayerHandicap[NEWLINE]Handicap for AI Players: %s3_AIPlayerHandicap
An extra-small World Size smaller than Base AdvCiv's Duel World Size. Requires the 48 civs DLL for default player counts.
An extra-large World Size sized for about 24 civs. Requires the 48 civs DLL for default player counts.
A massive World Size sized for about 32 civs. Requires the 48 civs DLL for default player counts.
A huge World Size sized for about 40 civs. Requires the 48 civs DLL for default player counts.
A huge World Size sized for about 48 civs. Requires the 48 civs DLL for default player counts.
Originally a SAS world-size helper, now kept as a playable flat-grass sandbox map (almost all land).
A faster game speed than Turbo: Research, growth, building and unit production take less time.
A faster game speed than Quick: Research, growth, building and unit production take less time.

Research, growth and production are faster than on normal speed. Still, as unit movement is unaffected by game speed, warfare is not necessarily that much faster. This setting is worth considering especially for multiplayer games and small maps.
This is the traditional game speed for Civ.
Research, growth and production take longer than on normal speed. As unit movement is unaffected by game speed, this setting allows more time for warfare in each era.
Less turns than in BTS and than in base AdvCiv. Research, growth, building and unit production take longer than on normal speed. This setting favors warfare and demands much patience.
A slower game speed than Marathon: Research, growth, building and unit production take longer.
A slower game speed than Slow: Research, growth, building and unit production take longer.

Significantly harder than base AdvCiv settings. This handicap setting still grants some advantages (such as in AI tech costs, build costs, unit costs, overall economy values, etc.; while the player's costs remain the same at all handicap settings unlike in base AdvCiv, for convenience of math and such), but AI should be expected to be quite merciless otherwise. There are no free techs nor AI free techs anymore in AdvCiv-SAS in any handicap setting. Some exceptions may be the starting point that is worst at lower handicap settings to best in highest handicap settings, for the player, to compensate the overall handicap.

Significantly harder than base AdvCiv settings and gradually harder than previous one. This handicap setting still grants some advantages but a bit less now, AI attitude should overall be the same or not too much harder otherwise.

Significantly harder than base AdvCiv settings and gradually harder than previous one. This handicap setting still grants some advantages but even less now. Warfare should start to feel even or if not should be close to it, vs barbarians but also other players.
Significantly harder than base AdvCiv settings and gradually harder than previous one. This is mostly an even game setting with other players, even though barbarians are still getting stronger.
Significantly harder than base AdvCiv settings and gradually harder than previous one. This handicap setting has significant penalties. Barbarians are also getting stronger.

Significantly harder than base AdvCiv settings and gradually harder than previous one. The penalties increase in this handicap setting. Economy and overall game plan needs to be really efficient and going forward on higher handicap settings to compensate AI advantages. Barbarians are also getting stronger.

Significantly harder than base AdvCiv settings and gradually harder than previous one. Efficiency and strategy may need to further be refined to be competitive at this handicap setting that has quite strong penalties. Barbarians are also getting stronger and may start to feel like being on a rampage if not at previous handicap settings, but this affects all players.

Should be significantly harder than base AdvCiv settings and gradually harder than previous one, but hopefully less of a grind. Penalties are consistently and gradually as well higher than in previous handicap settings, and same for barbarians settings, even though these affect all players as well. No drastic changes otherwise. Note: unlike in base AdvCiv, INCREASE_START_TURN is disabled, as there are no free techs nor ai free techs, so this means all players start at turn 1 at this handicap setting.

Should be significantly harder than base AdvCiv settings and gradually harder than previous one, but hopefully less of a grind. Should be very challenging still but gradually so ideally. If this is still too easy, i may add another or 2 other handicap settings gradually harder than this one based on player feedback even though this is not guaranteed but may be worthwile for deity players wanting a higher challenge. Note: unlike in base AdvCiv, INCREASE_START_TURN is disabled similarly so all players start at turn 1 at this handicap setting as well.
[TAB]Order a worker-type unit to construct a road on the selected tile.
[TAB]Order a worker-type unit to construct a railroad route on the selected tile.
[TAB]Order a worker-type unit to construct a Farm improvement on a suitable land tile.
[TAB]Order a work boat or similar unit to establish a fishing improvement on the selected water tile.
[TAB]Order a work boat or similar unit to establish a whaling improvement on the selected water tile.
[TAB]Order a worker-type unit to construct a Mine improvement on a suitable tile.
[TAB]Order a worker-type unit to construct a Workshop improvement on a suitable land tile.
[TAB]Order a worker-type unit to construct a Lumbermill improvement, usually on a forested tile.
[TAB]Order a worker-type unit to construct a Windmill improvement on a suitable tile.
[TAB]Order a worker-type unit to construct a Watermill improvement, commonly along rivers.
[TAB]Order a worker-type unit to construct a Plantation improvement on a suitable tile.
[TAB]Order a worker-type unit to construct a Quarry improvement on a suitable tile.
[TAB]Order a worker-type unit to construct a Pasture improvement on a suitable tile.
[TAB]Order a worker-type unit to construct a Camp improvement on a suitable tile.
[TAB]Order a worker-type unit to construct a Well improvement on a suitable tile.
[TAB]Order a work boat or similar unit to establish an offshore platform improvement on the selected water tile.
[TAB]Order a worker-type unit to construct a Cottage improvement on a suitable land tile.
[TAB]Order a worker-type unit to construct a Fort improvement on the selected land tile.
[TAB]Order a worker-type unit to clear jungle from the selected tile.
[TAB]Order a worker-type unit to cut down and remove forest from the selected tile.
[TAB]Order a worker-type unit to remove fallout contamination from the selected tile.
[TAB]Order a worker-type unit to establish a forest preserve improvement on a suitable tile.

Congratulations, you have trained your first Archery (Bow, Short) Unit ([COLOR_UNIT_TEXT]%s1Unit_Name[COLOR_REVERT]) in %s2City_Name! These units get a special first-strike ability that allows them a chance to hit their opponent before regular combat begins.

Congratulations, you have trained your first Archery (Bow, Long) Unit ([COLOR_UNIT_TEXT]%s1Unit_Name[COLOR_REVERT]) in %s2City_Name! These units get a special first-strike ability that allows them a chance to hit their opponent before regular combat begins.

Congratulations, you have trained your first Archery (Crossbow) Unit ([COLOR_UNIT_TEXT]%s1Unit_Name[COLOR_REVERT]) in %s2City_Name! These units get a special first-strike ability that allows them a chance to hit their opponent before regular combat begins.

Congratulations, you have trained your first Mounted (Melee) Unit ([COLOR_UNIT_TEXT]%s1Unit_Name[COLOR_REVERT]) in %s2City_Name! These units can move farther per turn than regular units, allowing them to wreak havoc behind enemy lines.

Congratulations, you have trained your first Mounted (Ranged) Unit ([COLOR_UNIT_TEXT]%s1Unit_Name[COLOR_REVERT]) in %s2City_Name! These units can move farther per turn than regular units, allowing them to wreak havoc behind enemy lines.
"If you speak the truth, have a foot in the stirrup." - Turkish proverb
"The Lord bless you and keep you; the Lord make His face to shine upon you and be gracious to you; the Lord lift up His countenance upon you and give you peace." - The Bible, Numbers
"A multitude of rulers is not a good thing. Let there be one ruler, one king." - Homer, The Iliad
"When I give food to the poor, they call me a saint. When I ask why the poor have no food, they call me a communist." - Dom Helder Camara
"One doesn't discover new lands without losing sight of the shore." - Andre Gide
"You can't direct the wind, but you can adjust your sails." - Unknown
"The wisest men follow their own direction." - ascribed to Euripides
"I do not feel obliged to believe that the same God who has endowed us with sense, reason, and intellect has intended us to forgo their use." - Galileo Galilei
"You would make a ship sail against the winds and currents by lighting a bon-fire under her deck? I have no time for such nonsense." - Napoleon, on Robert Fulton's Steamship (anecdote)
"It is not the strongest of the species that survive, but the one most responsive to change." - Charles Darwin (paraphrase)
"There is a single light of science, and to brighten it anywhere is to brighten it everywhere." - Isaac Asimov
"I fooled you, I fooled you, I got pig iron, I got pig iron, I got all pig iron." - Lonnie Donegan, "Rock Island Line"
"Two cities have been formed by two loves: the earthly by the love of self; the heavenly by the love of God." - St. Augustine
"The future will be better tomorrow." - Dan Quayle
"The whole is more than the sum of its parts." - Aristotle
"Any sufficiently advanced technology is indistinguishable from magic." - Arthur C. Clarke
"Some books are to be tasted, others to be swallowed, and some few to be chewed and digested." - Sir Francis Bacon
"The whole is greater than the sum of its parts." - Aristotle
"I just want to say one word to you. Just one word: plastics." - Calder Willingham, The Graduate
"The only thing worse than being talked about is not being talked about." - Oscar Wilde
"The great masses of the people... will more easily fall victims to a big lie than to a small one." - Adolf Hitler
"The real problem is not whether machines think, but whether men do." - B.F. Skinner
"Behind a veil, unseen yet present, I was the forceful soul that moved this mighty body." - Jean Racine
"Words have the power to both destroy and heal. When words are both true and kind, they can change our world." - ascribed to the Buddha
"People of the same trade seldom meet together, even for merriment and diversion, but the conversation ends in a conspiracy against the public." - Adam Smith
"Do not throw the arrow which will return against you." - Kurdish Proverb
"I will to my lord be true and faithful, and love all which he loves and shun all which he shuns." - Anglo Saxon Oath of Fealty
"Nature herself has imprinted on the minds of all the idea of God." - Cicero
"Nothing travels faster than the speed of light with the possible exception of bad news, which obeys its own special laws." - Douglas Adams
"Everything in life is somewhere else, and you get there in a car." - E. B. White
"I am the state." - ascribed to Louis XIV
"And on the pedestal these words appear: 'My name is Ozymandias, king of kings: Look on my works, ye Mighty, and despair!' Nothing beside remains." - Percy Bysshe Shelley
"Before that steam drill shall beat me down, I'll die with my hammer in my hand." - from "John Henry, the Steel-Driving Man"
"And them that take the sword shall perish by the sword." - The Bible, Matthew
"I must study politics and war that my sons may have liberty to study mathematics and philosophy." - John Adams
"Compound interest is the most powerful force in the universe." - ascribed to Albert Einstein
"What is happiness? The feeling that power is growing, that resistance is overcome." - Friedrich Nietzsche
"Political power grows out of the barrel of a gun." - Mao Zedong
"There is no wealth like knowledge, no poverty like ignorance." - Ali ibn Abi-Talib
"All the world's a stage, And all the men and women merely players. They have their exits and their entrances; And one man in his time plays many parts." - William Shakespeare
"If music be the food of love, play on." - William Shakespeare
"I cannot live without books" - Thomas Jefferson
"Tell me what you eat, and I will tell you what you are." - Anthelme Brillat-Savarin
"Victorious warriors win first and then go to war, while defeated warriors go to war first and then seek to win." - Sun-Tzu
I am pleased to meet you, [CT_NAME]! Let us each slaughter fifty thousand slaves to celebrate this auspicious occasion.
TEXT.French: C'est un plaisir de faire votre connaissance, [CT_NAME] ! Sacrifions tous deux cinq mille esclaves pour célébrer cette rencontre de bon augure.
TEXT.German: Ich freue mich, Euch kennen zu lernen, [CT_NAME]! Lasst uns zur Feier dieses denkwürdigen Augenblicks je fünfzigtausend Sklaven opfern!
TEXT.Italian: Piacere di incontrarti, [CT_NAME]! Per festeggiare questo momento propizio, dovremmo massacrare cinquantamila schiavi ciascuno.
TEXT.Spanish: Me alegro mucho de conoceros, [CT_NAME]. Sacrifiquemos cada uno a cincuenta mil esclavos para celebrar esta propicia ocasión.

TEXT.French: Parlez plutôt à ma lame de cérémonie, [CT_NAME].
TEXT.German: Ihr könnt gern Bekanntschaft mit meiner Klinge machen, [CT_NAME]!
TEXT.Italian: Perché non ne discuti con la lama cerimoniale, [CT_NAME]?

TEXT.English: Tell me, [CT_NAME]: what has [CT_CIV_SHORT] done to anger the gods so that they give them you as leader?
TEXT.French: Dites-moi, [CT_NAME]... Qu'ont bien pu faire les [CT_CIV_SHORT] pour que les dieux soient irrités au point de vous nommer à leur tête ?
TEXT.German: Sagt mir, [CT_NAME]: Wie konnte [CT_CIV_SHORT] die Götter nur so erzürnen, dass sie ihm Euch als Staatsoberhaupt vorgesetzt haben?
TEXT.Italian: Dimmi, [CT_NAME], cosa ha fatto [CT_CIV_SHORT:2] perché gli dei si siano adirati a tal punto da fornire loro un leader come te?
TEXT.Spanish: Decidnos, [CT_NAME], ¿qué ha hecho [CT_CIV_SHORT] para enojar a los dioses y que os convirtieran en su líder?

TEXT.English: Your weakness is contemptible, [CT_NAME]! Give us what we want or perish!
TEXT.French: Votre faiblesse est méprisable, [CT_NAME] ! Donnez-nous ce que nous voulons ou ce sera la mort !
TEXT.German: Ihr seid unsäglich jämmerlich und schwach, [CT_NAME]! Gebt uns, was wir verlangen, oder sterbt!
TEXT.Italian: La tua debolezza è imbarazzante, [CT_NAME]! Dacci ciò che vogliamo o morirai!
TEXT.Spanish: ¡Vuestra debilidad es despreciable, [CT_NAME]! ¡Dadnos lo que queremos o pereced!

TEXT.English: I am [OUR_NAME], Oba of Benin. You enter my court, [CT_NAME]. Speak with respect, and we may prosper.
Your words offend the throne, [CT_NAME]. Return when you can speak with honor.
Peace is not yet yours, [CT_NAME]. The debt between us remains unpaid.
Welcome, [CT_NAME]. Benin remembers those who keep their word.
You stand before me, [CT_NAME], yet you bring only trouble.
You are in no position to refuse, [CT_NAME]. Send tribute, and keep your people safe.
Tribute buys stability, [CT_NAME]. Pay, and we will have no quarrel.
Even the mighty benefit from order, [CT_NAME]. Honor my court with tribute, and we both avoid wasteful war.
Ahoy, [CT_NAME]. I am [OUR_NAME]. Speak your business, and be quick about it.
I have heard enough from you, [CT_NAME]. Leave.
No peace yet, [CT_NAME]. You have not earned it.
Fair winds, [CT_NAME]. Your word has held.
We have little to discuss, [CT_NAME]. Choose your next words carefully.
You think me weak, [CT_NAME]? Then prove it. Pay, or face me.
A fair tribute, [CT_NAME], and we avoid trouble.
Pay for safe passage, [CT_NAME]. My ships are not charity.
I am [OUR_NAME]. We will speak plainly, [CT_NAME]. State your purpose.
Not now, [CT_NAME]. I have nothing to say to you.
Peace comes when you stop forcing my hand, [CT_NAME].
Good to see you, [CT_NAME]. Keep it practical.
Enough games, [CT_NAME]. You are making yourself a problem.
Do not mistake patience for weakness, [CT_NAME]. Give what I ask.
A contribution now, [CT_NAME], prevents trouble later.
Pay, [CT_NAME]. I would rather end this cleanly.
Prereq event must be in the same city
Other player gets a response choice
